info.title: DevLog+
info.description:
Single-user developer journal for technical learning and skill maintenance.

DevLog+ has two engines:

- **Learning engine** — builds a Knowledge Profile from journal entries, quizzes, and feedback. Understands what you know, where you're weak, and what to explore next.
- **Practice engine** — generates weekly Go micro-projects calibrated to your practical level.

## Core workflow

1. **Journal** — write technical entries (typed or dictated) about what you learn.
2. **Profile** — the system builds and maintains a Knowledge Profile nightly.
3. **Quiz** — weekly free-text quizzes probe your understanding.
4. **Readings** — curated reading recommendations from your allowlisted domains.
5. **Projects** — weekly Go micro-projects with bugs, features, and refactors.
6. **Feedback** — thumbs-up/down reactions and feedforward notes steer future content.
7. **Triage** — items the system can't resolve are surfaced for your clarification.

post.description:
Create a new technical journal entry.

The entry content can be typed text or dictated via the browser Web Speech
API.  An initial version is created automatically.  The entry will be
processed by the nightly profile-update pipeline.

422.description: Validation error — content is required and must be non-empty

get.description:
Return journal entries ordered by creation date (most recent first).

Supports offset/limit pagination. Response is wrapped in a
:class:`PaginatedResponse` so clients (notably AI agents) can tell when
they've reached the last page without an extra speculative request.
Each entry includes its latest content snapshot.

200.description: Paginated envelope of journal entries, most recent first

get.summary: Get journal entry with version history
get.description:
Retrieve a single journal entry including its full version history.

Versions are append-only; the most recent version is the source of truth.

200.description: The journal entry with all historical versions

put.description:
Edit a journal entry.

Creates a new version instead of overwriting — the previous content is
preserved in the version history.  The new version becomes the source of
truth for future profile updates.

200.description: The updated journal entry (new version created)

delete.description: Permanently delete a journal entry and all of its versions.

200.description: Confirmation message

get.description:
Return the current Knowledge Profile.

The profile is an AI-derived, read-only view of the user's technical
knowledge.  Topics are organized into:

- **strengths** — topics with strong evidence
- **weak_spots** — topics with limited evidence
- **current_frontier** — topics actively being learned
- **next_frontier** — adjacent topics recommended for exploration
- **recurring_themes** — frequently mentioned topics
- **unresolved** — topics pending triage

Updated nightly by the profile-update pipeline.

200.description: The full Knowledge Profile organized by evidence strength

get.description:
List historical profile snapshots in reverse-chronological order.

Each snapshot captures the full Knowledge Profile state at a point in time,
enabling the user to track knowledge growth over weeks and months.

200.description: Paginated list of historical profile snapshots

schema.title: Response List Snapshots Api V1 Profile Snapshots Get

get.description:
Return quiz sessions ordered by creation date (most recent first).

Each session represents a weekly quiz with a configurable number of
free-text questions generated from the Knowledge Profile.

200.description: Paginated list of quiz sessions, most recent first

schema.title: Response List Sessions Api V1 Quizzes Sessions Get

get.description:
Get the active (non-evaluated) quiz session, if any.

Returns `null` when there is no pending session.  A new session is
generated weekly by the quiz pipeline.

200.description: The active quiz session with questions, or null if none exists

schema.title: Response Get Current Session Api V1 Quizzes Sessions Current Get

get.description:
Retrieve a specific quiz session with its questions, submitted answers,
and LLM-generated evaluations.

Evaluations include correctness (full / partial / incorrect), depth
assessment, explanation, and confidence score.

200.description: Quiz session with all questions, answers, and evaluations

post.description:
Submit a free-text answer to a quiz question.

Only free-text answers are accepted (no multiple choice).  Each question
can only be answered once.  The answer will be evaluated by an LLM judge
when the session is completed.

Mark a quiz session as completed.

All answers should be submitted before completing.  Once completed, the
quiz evaluation pipeline will assess each answer and update the Knowledge
Profile accordingly.

200.description: The completed quiz session

get.description:
Return reading recommendations ordered by batch date (most recent first).

Recommendations are generated weekly from the Knowledge Profile and limited
to domains on the user's allowlist.  Each batch typically contains 3–5 links.
The response is wrapped in a :class:`PaginatedResponse` envelope so agent
clients know the full size of the archive in a single request.

200.description: Paginated envelope of reading recommendations, most recent batch first

get.description:
Return every domain on the reading allowlist.

The allowlist controls which domains the reading-generation pipeline may
link to.  Default entries are pre-populated on first run.

200.description: All domains currently on the reading allowlist

schema.title: Response List Allowlist Api V1 Readings Allowlist Get

post.description:
Add a new domain to the reading allowlist.

Once added, the reading-generation pipeline may include links from this
domain in future weekly recommendations.

422.description: Validation error — domain and name are required

put.description: Update the name or description of an existing allowlist entry.

200.description: The updated allowlist entry

delete.description:
Remove a domain from the reading allowlist.

Future reading recommendations will no longer include links from this
domain.  Existing recommendations are not affected.

200.description: Confirmation message

get.description:
Return weekly projects ordered by issue date (most recent first).

Each project is a self-contained Go codebase with source files, tests,
and a set of tasks (bugs, features, refactors, optimizations).

200.description: Paginated list of weekly Go projects, most recent first

schema.title: Response List Projects Api V1 Projects Get

get.description:
Get the most recently issued weekly project.

Returns `null` when no project has been issued yet.  Includes the full
task list, project metadata, and evaluation (if submitted).

200.description: The most recently issued project with tasks and evaluation, or null

schema.title: Response Get Current Project Api V1 Projects Current Get

get.description: Retrieve a specific weekly project with its tasks and evaluation.

200.description: Project with tasks, metadata, and evaluation

post.description:
Submit a completed project for AI evaluation.

The project evaluation pipeline will assess code quality, task completion,
and test results.  The evaluation must complete before the next weekly
project is issued.

200.description: The project with updated status

404.description: Project not found or not in a submittable state

get.description:
Return triage items with optional status and severity filters.

Triage items are created automatically by the profile-update,
quiz-evaluation, and project-evaluation pipelines when the system
encounters ambiguity it cannot resolve on its own. The response is
wrapped in a :class:`PaginatedResponse` envelope so agent clients can
page through the full queue deterministically.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status (pending, accepted, rejected, edited, deferred)
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status (pending, accepted, rejected, edited, deferred)

parameters.name: severity

schema.description: Filter by severity (low, medium, high, critical)
schema.title: Severity

parameters.description: Filter by severity (low, medium, high, critical)

200.description: Paginated envelope of triage items matching the optional filters

get.description:
Check whether unresolved high or critical triage items exist.

When blocking items are present, the nightly profile-update pipeline
will not run until they are resolved.  The frontend should surface a
prominent warning in this case.

200.description: Object with a single boolean field `blocking`

schema.title: Response Check Blocking Triage Api V1 Triage Blocking Get

get.description: Retrieve a specific triage item by ID.

200.description: A single triage item with full details

post.description:
Resolve a triage item with a user action and optional clarification.

Allowed actions: `accepted`, `rejected`, `edited`, `deferred`.  When the
action is `edited`, `resolution_text` should contain the user's
clarifying input.

200.description: The resolved triage item

post.description:
Submit feedback and/or feedforward on a generated item.

Two distinct signal types are supported:

- **Feedback** (`reaction`) — a thumbs-up or thumbs-down that corrects
  the system's current understanding (e.g. “too easy”, “not relevant”).
- **Feedforward** (`note`) — free-text that shapes what the system should
  generate next (e.g. “more backend-oriented content”).

At least one of `reaction` or `note` should be provided.

422.description: Validation error — target_type and target_id are required

get.description: Return all feedback entries ordered by creation date (most recent first).

200.description: Paginated list of all feedback entries, most recent first

schema.title: Response List Feedback Api V1 Feedback Get

get.description:
Return every application setting as a key-value pair.

Settings include model selection, quiz question count, cron schedules,
and other tunables.  Values are stored as JSON objects.

200.description: All stored application settings

schema.title: Response List Settings Api V1 Settings Get

get.description: Retrieve a single setting by its unique key.

200.description: The requested setting

put.description:
Create a new setting or update an existing one.

The `value` field accepts any JSON object.  If the key already exists,
its value is replaced.

Keys that control LLM model selection or credentials (``llm_model_*``,
``openrouter_*``, ``langfuse_*``, ``database_url``, …) are reserved for
the ``.env`` configuration and will be rejected with 403 — this prevents
the database (and export bundles) from becoming a surface for altering
security-sensitive configuration.

200.description: The created or updated setting

403.description: Key is reserved for environment variables (e.g. LLM model selection, API credentials) and cannot be set through the API.

get.description:
Check whether the first-run onboarding has been completed.

Returns `{"completed": true}` after the onboarding flow finishes.
The frontend should redirect to the onboarding wizard when `false`.

200.description: Object with a single boolean field `completed`

schema.title: Response Get Onboarding Status Api V1 Onboarding Status Get

get.description:
Retrieve the full onboarding state.

Returns the self-assessment answers, Go experience level, topic interests,
and completion timestamp.  Returns empty/default values if onboarding
has not started.

200.description: Full onboarding state including self-assessment data

post.description:
Complete the first-run onboarding flow (~10–15 min).

Collects:
- **Self-assessment** — technical background, languages, experience
- **Go experience level** — none / beginner / intermediate / advanced
- **Topic interests** — optional list of topics to prioritise

This establishes baseline context before the normal nightly/weekly
processing cycles begin.

200.description: The completed onboarding state

422.description: Validation error — self_assessment and go_experience required

get.description: Downloads a JSON bundle containing every table's data. Use this to move your DevLog+ data to another machine. Embeddings and processing logs are excluded — they will be regenerated automatically on the new machine.

200.description: Successful Response

post.summary: Import application data from an export bundle
post.description: Upload a JSON export file produced by the /export endpoint. **This is destructive** — all existing data will be replaced with the contents of the uploaded file. Intended for migrating your DevLog+ instance to a new machine.

parameters.name: confirm_overwrite

schema.description: Must be true when importing into a database that already contains data. Prevents accidental overwrites.
schema.title: Confirm Overwrite

parameters.description: Must be true when importing into a database that already contains data. Prevents accidental overwrites.

200.description: Successful Response

get.summary: Preview export metadata without downloading the full bundle
get.description: Returns table row counts and metadata so you can verify what will be exported before triggering the full download.

200.description: Successful Response

post.description:
Runs the nightly profile-update pipeline on demand. Normally this runs automatically at 2:00 AM via cron; use this endpoint when you don't want to wait.

The pipeline runs in the background — the response returns immediately with status=queued. Poll `GET /pipelines/runs` to observe progress.
Generates a new weekly quiz session immediately rather than waiting for the Monday 3:00 AM cron. Runs in the background; poll `GET /pipelines/runs` for progress.
Generates a new weekly batch of reading recommendations immediately rather than waiting for the Monday 3:30 AM cron. Runs in the background; poll `GET /pipelines/runs` for progress.

Generates a new weekly Go micro-project immediately rather than waiting for the Monday 4:00 AM cron. Runs in the background; poll `GET /pipelines/runs` for progress. Note: generates files under `workspace/projects/<date>/`.

get.description: Returns the most recent entries from the processing log, newest first. Useful for displaying the status of manually-triggered or scheduled pipeline runs in the UI.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of runs to return (newest first).
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return (newest first).

parameters.name: pipeline

schema.description: Optional filter — return only runs of a given pipeline.
schema.title: Pipeline

parameters.description: Optional filter — return only runs of a given pipeline.

200.description: Successful Response

schema.title: Response List Runs Api V1 Pipelines Runs Get

description.description: Optional description of what this source provides

tags.name: journal
tags.description: CRUD operations for technical journal entries. Entries are text-only (typed or dictated via browser Web Speech API). Edits are versioned — the most recent version is the source of truth. New entries are processed nightly by the profile-update pipeline.

tags.name: profile
tags.description: Read-only view of the AI-derived Knowledge Profile. The profile organizes topics by evidence strength (strong / developing / limited), surfaces the current frontier (topics actively being learned) and the next frontier (recommended adjacent topics). Updated nightly.

tags.name: quizzes
tags.description: Weekly free-text quizzes that probe understanding. Each session has 10 questions (configurable). Answers are evaluated by an LLM judge for correctness (full / partial / incorrect), depth, and confidence. Results feed back into the Knowledge Profile.

tags.name: readings
tags.description: Weekly reading recommendations and domain allowlist management. Recommendations are generated from the Knowledge Profile and limited to domains on the user's allowlist. The allowlist ships with sensible defaults and is fully editable.

tags.name: projects
tags.description: Weekly LLM-generated Go micro-projects. Each project is a self-contained codebase with source, tests, tasks (bugs, features, refactors, optimizations), and a README. Submit your solution and receive an AI evaluation before the next project is issued.

tags.name: triage
tags.description: Items the system can't confidently resolve on its own. Created by the profile-update, quiz-evaluation, and project-evaluation pipelines. Severity levels: low / medium / high / critical. High and critical items block the next profile-update cycle until resolved.

tags.name: feedback
tags.description:
Two distinct signal types on every generated item:

- **Feedback** (thumbs-up / thumbs-down) — corrects the system's current understanding.
- **Feedforward** (free-text note) — shapes what the system should generate next.

Attach to individual quiz questions, readings, or the project as a whole.

tags.name: settings
tags.description: User-configurable application settings stored as key-value pairs. Includes model selection, quiz question count, cron schedules, and other tunables.

tags.name: onboarding
tags.description: First-run experience (~10–15 min). Collects technical background self-assessment, Go experience level, a short diagnostic quiz, and optional topic interests. Establishes baseline context before the normal processing cycle begins.

tags.name: transfer
tags.description: Export and import all application data for device-to-device migration. Export produces a single JSON file containing every table. Import replaces all existing data with the uploaded bundle — use when moving DevLog+ from one machine to another.

tags.name: pipelines
tags.description: Manual, user-initiated triggers for the pipelines that normally run on a cron schedule (profile update, quiz, reading, and project generation). Intended as an opt-in escape hatch exposed in the Settings page — not part of the normal daily flow. Each trigger returns 202 Accepted immediately and runs the pipeline in the background; use `GET /pipelines/runs` to observe progress.
